person.name: William Carey

popularStory.headline: History of William Carey (Courtier)
popularStory.description:
Taken from Wikipedia
William Carey, of Aldenham, in Hertfordshire (c. 1500 – 22 June 1528) was a courtier and favourite of King Henry VIII of England. He served the king as a Gentleman of the Privy ch …

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
William Carey, of Aldenham, in Hertfordshire (c.?1500-22 June 1528) was a courtier and favourite of King Henry VIII of England. He served the king as a Gentleman of the Privy chamber, and Esquire of the Body to the King. His wife, Mary Boleyn, is known to history as a mistress of King Henry VIII and the sister of Henry's second wife, Anne Boleyn.

«b»Biography«/b»
William Carey was the second son of Sir Thomas Carey (1455-1500), of Chilton Foliat in Wiltshire, and his wife, Margaret Spencer, daughter of Sir Robert Spencer and Eleanor Beaufort, and grandson of Sir William Cary of Cockington, Devon, an eminent Lancastrian. This Cary family were anciently recorded in Devon, and originally held the manors at Cockington and Clovelly in that county. Eleanor was the daughter of Edmund Beaufort, 2nd Duke of Somerset, whose brother John Beaufort, 1st Duke of Somerset, was the father of Margaret Beaufort, Countess of Richmond and Derby, grandmother of King Henry VIII; thus William and Henry VIII were third cousins. William's maternal aunt was Catherine Spencer, Countess of Northumberland, and through her, he was first cousin to Henry Percy, 6th Earl of Northumberland, the former suitor of his sister-in-law Anne Boleyn.

On 4 February 1520, he was married to Mary Boleyn, the elder daughter of Thomas Boleyn, 1st Earl of Wiltshire and his wife, Lady Elizabeth Howard. They resided at Aldenham in Hertfordshire.

Shortly after their marriage, Mary became the mistress of King Henry VIII. The Boleyns received grants of land, and Carey himself profited from his wife's unfaithfulness, being granted manors and estates by the King while it was in progress. Carey was also a noted art collector and he introduced the famed Dutch artist, Lucas Horenbout, to the Kingdom of England in the mid-1520s. Perhaps one of the reasons the athletic King Henry VIII favoured Carey was the fact that Carey appears to have been fond of activities such as riding, hunting and jousting. Carey distinguished himself in jousting at the Field of the Cloth of Gold in 1520.

Anne Boleyn, Mary's sister, caught Henry's eye a year after his affair with Mary ended. Henry proposed marriage to her in 1527. William Carey did not live to enjoy his sister-in-law's prosperity, since he died of the sweating sickness the following year. Brian Tuke, Henry's secretary at the time of Carey's death wrote this to Lord Legat the day after his death: "Now is word common that M. Cary, which before I came lay in the chamber where I lie, and with whom at my first coming I met here in this place, saying that he had been with his wife at Plashey, and would not be seen within, because he would ride again and hunt, is dead of the sweat. Our Lord have mercy on his soul; and hold his hand over us." He died greatly in debt, and his wife was reduced to pawning her jewellery before Anne Boleyn arranged a pension for her.

«b»Children of William Carey and Mary Boleyn«/b»
William Carey and Mary Boleyn were the parents of two children:

1.) Catherine Carey (c. 1524-15 January 1568). Maid of Honour to Anne of Cleves and Catherine Howard. She was married to the Puritan Sir Francis Knollys, Knight of the Garter. She was later lady-in-waiting to her cousin, Elizabeth I. One of her daughters, Lettice Knollys, became the second wife of Robert Dudley, 1st Earl of Leicester, the favourite of Elizabeth I.

2.) Henry Carey, 1st Baron Hunsdon (4 March 1526-23 July 1596). He was ennobled by Queen Elizabeth I just after her coronation and created Knight of the Garter in 1561. When Henry was dying, Elizabeth offered him the Boleyn family title, Earl of Ormonde, which he had long sought, but he refused the honour.

Because of Mary's affair, it has been suggested that Catherine and Henry may have been instead Henry VIII's biological children (see Issue of Mary Boleyn). The veracity of this claim is the subject of historical debate.

sourceList.title: William Carey in entry for George, Lord Hunsdon Carey, "Ireland Births and Baptisms, 1620-1881"

zh.lifeSummary: William Carey是Thomas Carey和Margaret Spencer的兒子，於1489年出生在英國維爾爾。他於1520年2月4日與Mary Boleyn在英國英格蘭倫敦格林維其結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1528年6月22日在英國格盧斯特斯爾享年39歲，埋葬在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: William Carey是Thomas Carey和Margaret Spencer的儿子，于1489年出生在英国維爾爾。他于1520年2月4日与Mary Boleyn在英国英格兰倫敦格林維其结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1528年6月22日在英国格盧斯特斯爾享年39岁，埋葬在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Carey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Ciardha, a midland surname meaning ‘descendant of Ciardha’, a personal name derived from ciar ‘dark, black’. See Kerry .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhiachra ‘son of Fiachra’.

English: from Carey Barton in Devon or Castle Cary, Lytes Cary, Cary Fitzpaine, or Babcary, all in Somerset, named for the rivers on which they stand; both river names probably derive from the Celtic root car- ‘love, liking’, perhaps with the meaning ‘pleasant stream’.

variants.name: Cary
meanings: Irish (Donegal and Westmeath) and English: variant of   Carey  .

variants.name: Karey
meanings: English (London) and Irish: variant of   Carey  .

variants.name: Kary
meanings: Irish: variant of   Carey  . This surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Keary
meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Ciardha (from a personal name based on ciar ‘black’), sometimes Anglicized as Carey .

variants.name: Kerry
meanings: English: from the rare Middle English personal name Kari (Old Norse Kári).
Irish: from Ó Ciardha, ‘descendant of Ciardha’, a personal name meaning ‘the black one’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: William Carey was born in 1489, in Wiltshire, England as the son of Thomas Carey and Margaret Spencer. He married Mary Boleyn on 4 February 1520, in Greenwich, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 22 June 1528, in Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 39, and was buried in England.

fr.lifeSummary: William Carey est né en 1489, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, fils de Thomas Carey et Margaret Spencer. Il a épousé Mary Boleyn le 4 février 1520, à Greenwich, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 22 juin 1528, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: William Carey wurde 1489 in Wiltshire, England, als Sohn von Thomas Carey und Margaret Spencer geboren. Er hat am 4. Februar 1520 in Greenwich, London, England, United Kingdom Mary Boleyn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 22. Juni 1528 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Gloucestershire, England und wurde in England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: William Carey è nato il nel 1489 nel Wiltshire, England come figlio di Thomas Carey e Margaret Spencer. Lui sposato Mary Boleyn il 4 febbraio 1520, nel Greenwich, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 22 giugno 1528, nel Gloucestershire, England, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolto a England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Careyは1489年にイギリスウィルトシュアイアでThomas CareyとMargaret Spencerの息子として生まれた。1520年2月4日にイギリスイギリスロンドングレエンウィチでMary Boleynと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1528年6月22日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアで39歳で死去し，イギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Carey(은)는 1489년 영국 윌츠히르에서 Thomas Carey(와)과 Margaret Spencer의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1520년 2월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 그린위치에서 Mary Boleyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1528년 6월 22일 영국 그루스스터실르에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국에 묻혔다.

mn.Chapel Royal, Greenwich Palace, London: Greenwich, London, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: William Carey nasceu em 1489, em Wiltshire, Inglaterra filho de Thomas Carey e Margaret Spencer. Ele casou-se com Mary Boleyn em 4 de fevereiro de 1520, em Greenwich, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 22 de junho de 1528, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, com 39 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: William Carey родился в 1489 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия. Его родителей звали Thomas Carey и Margaret Spencer. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Boleyn 4 февраля 1520 г. в местности гринвич, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 22 июня 1528 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: William Carey nació en 1489, en Wiltshire, Inglaterra como hijo de Thomas Carey y Margaret Spencer. Se casó con Mary Boleyn el 4 de febrero de 1520, en Greenwich, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 22 de junio de 1528, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra.
